lessons.title: What is capacity modeling?
lessons.lede: This workbench turns "how busy is something, over time?" into a picture you can read at a glance — for a person, a team, a company, or anything else with limits.

lessons.title: Reading the heatmap
lessons.lede: Lesson 1 said what a heatmap is. This one is about reading a finished one: what each colour is telling you, day by day.

lessons.title: Two modes: Subtractive vs Additive
lessons.lede: The core fork of the whole tool. Everything about a heatmap is shared except one choice: what sets the point that maps to full red.

lessons.title: From raw to effective capacity
lessons.lede: The number you declare is rarely the room you actually have. Holidays, meetings and part-time patterns eat into it. This lesson turns raw declared capacity into the real Reference that Subtractive mode paints against.

lessons.title: Modeling demand: consumers & load shapes
lessons.lede: Capacity is the room; demand is what fills it. Here you learn how load gets added to a day — the two kinds of thing that generate it, and how their amounts pile up when they overlap.

lessons.title: Utilisation and the 100% trap
lessons.lede: A heatmap that's solid deep-red is a warning light, not a trophy. Being totally full is fragile, not efficient — aim for mostly warm, not scalding.

lessons.title: Composition & constraints: do sources add up, or does the weakest link win?
lessons.lede: The moment a Subject has more than one capacity source, you face one fork: do the sources sum, or is one of them a bottleneck? Getting it wrong makes the heatmap confidently lie.

lessons.title: Scaling: person → team → company
lessons.lede: One model, every level. The same coloured-squares picture works for a single person, a whole team, or the entire company — you just choose how far to zoom out. Nothing new to learn; only what to add together.

lessons.title: Capstone: model a scenario end-to-end
lessons.lede: Everything in one build. We'll take a real situation — a small marketing team facing a big launch — and turn it into a single, trustworthy heatmap, one decision at a time.

reference.title: Glossary
reference.lede: The shared vocabulary of the Capacity Heatmap Modeler. Every lesson uses these words precisely — this page is the source of truth. Mirrors the project CONTEXT.md.
